summary: Verified the just-rewritten /app/frontend/src/components/hos/MultiDayRunner.js by deep source-code review against /app/frontend/src/lib/hosAdvancedScenarios.js MULTIDAY_SCENARIOS (M1-M4). The deriveShifts() helper at the bottom of the file correctly merges per-day data into a flat shifts[] for all 4 scenarios. Live Playwright drill could not complete because the badge/PIN login flow refused to authenticate this Playwright session (page went blank after typing badge+continue, even with keyboard.type firing React onChange) — see action items.
verified_via_source_review: deriveShifts(days) at lines 530-580 of MultiDayRunner.js — per-scenario trace:

M1 (priorDay-overnight ending on Day 1, then contained Day 2): Day 1 has continuesFromPrev=true but `open` is null (no Day-0 open) → falls through and creates contained s = {Day1 00:00 → Day1 04:30}. Day 2 has no flags → s = {Day2 06:00 → Day2 16:00}. Result: 2 shifts. Matches review-request expectation.

M2 (overnight Day1→Day2, no violation): Day 1 continuesToNext → s = {Day1 18:00 → Day1 24:00} held as `open`. Day 2 continuesFromPrev → close: open.endDay=2, open.endMin=5*60+30=330 → push {Day1 18:00 → Day2 05:30} as ONE shift. Result: 1 merged shift. Matches CRITICAL requirement.

M3 (overnight Day1→Day2, BOTH violations): Day 1 continuesToNext → s = {Day1 15:00 → Day1 24:00} held. Day 2 continuesFromPrev with violation11/14=true and regulatoryEndMin=300 → close: endDay=2, endMin=360, violation11/14=true, regulatoryEndDay=2, regulatoryEndMin=300, explanation prefers Day-2 strings (where violation surfaces). Result: 1 merged shift carrying violations on the merge.

M4 (two contained day shifts): Day 1 no continues* flags → push {Day1 05:00 → Day1 18:30, violation11=true}. Day 2 no flags → push {Day2 03:00 → Day2 13:00}. Result: 2 separate shifts — does NOT merge (correct; no continuesToNext flag).
ShiftQuestionCard (lines 317-348) prompt text: 'When did this work shift START and END?' — neutral, no scenario count leak ('Shift 1 of N' is absent from the active card).

AnotherShiftCard (lines 433-453) prompt: 'Is there another work shift in these two days that you haven't identified yet?' — neutral, no 'last shift'/'final shift'/'only shift' wording. Both Yes/No buttons rendered with correct testids `${testid}-yes` / `${testid}-no`.

DaySection (lines 298-315) builds testid as `day-section-${label.toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, '-')}` → for labels 'Day 1' / 'Day 2' produces day-section-day-1 / day-section-day-2 (matches review-request selectors).
ContextDayStrip with prior-day-strip testid renders above grids when scenario.priorDayLog exists — present in all 4 MULTIDAY_SCENARIOS.

Active draggable handles are attached to BOTH grids simultaneously (buildMarkersForDay branches on tStart.day === dayN and tEnd.day === dayN), so the inspector can drag END onto Day 2 while START stays on Day 1 — supporting the overnight single-shift UX.

Violation grading: ViolationQuestionCard derives correctValue from shift.violation11/violation14. For merged M3 shift the merged flags carry both true → correctValue='both' → clicking violation-0-both renders the green '✓ Correct' feedback box.

Toolbar testids present: multiday-pick-scenario-btn, multiday-restart-btn, multiday-next-scenario-btn, multiday-scenario-pick-{i}. resetAll() correctly resets shiftIdx, shiftAnswers, phase, and handles.
Day-after strip is gated behind allShiftsDone (line 266) — does NOT leak the answer prematurely.
ResultsSummary tolerance: |userMin - canonicalMin| <= 10 with day match — typed times (round 5-min) will match exactly so '✓' renders for all four expected drills.
live_test_blocker: Playwright login flow: typing badge '042' (via page.keyboard.type with delay) and clicking [data-testid=badge-continue-btn] resulted in a BLANK page (only Emergent badge visible) — no PIN screen, no error. This was reproducible across 3 attempts. Direct navigation to /hos-practice without auth bounces back to /login. Iteration 37 confirmed the login flow works in production, so this is likely a Playwright session/storage quirk in this run, NOT a regression in login UI. Main agent can verify in browser directly.
action_items: Main agent: do a quick manual smoke in the live preview (login Badge 042 / PIN 1234 → /hos-practice?cat=multiday → wait 5s past splash) to visually confirm M2/M3 single-shift overnight UX. Source review confirms the merge logic is correct for all 4 scenarios.

Optional polish: deriveShifts() does not consult a `priorDay` open shift for M1 (where Day 1 has continuesFromPrev=true but the prior shift lives in priorDayLog, not days[]). The fallthrough creates a contained Day 1 shift {00:00→04:30} which is what the review-request expects, so functionally correct — but the comment at line 537 ('Close the open overnight shift on this day') is dead code on M1's first day. Consider documenting that priorDay continuation is intentionally treated as a Day-1-bounded shift.

Code-quality: MultiDayRunner.js is 581 lines — within the 700-line guideline but ContextDayStrip is still duplicated verbatim in EightDayRunner.js (~13 lines). Extract to /app/frontend/src/components/hos/ContextDayStrip.js when convenient.
critical_code_review_comments: deriveShifts() in MultiDayRunner.js correctly produces: M1→2 shifts, M2→1 merged shift, M3→1 merged shift carrying both violations, M4→2 separate shifts. Cross-midnight overnight shifts (continuesToNext + continuesFromPrev) are merged into ONE entry as required.
UI never reveals scenario count on the active question card; AnotherShiftCard wording is neutral on both overnight and contained scenarios.

data-testid coverage is complete: day-section-day-1/2, prior-day-strip, shift-{i}, shift-{i}-{start|end}{|-day}, shift-{i}-submit, violation-{i}-{none|11|14|both}, violation-{i}-next, another-{i}-{yes|no}, multi-results, multiday-{restart|next-scenario|pick-scenario}-btn, multiday-scenario-pick-{i}.

Minor: regulatoryEndDay/regulatoryEndMin propagation in deriveShifts() correctly carries to the merged shift for M3 (Day 2 regulatoryEndMin=300 → merged.regulatoryEndDay=2, merged.regulatoryEndMin=300). The buildMarkersForDay ResultSummary 'SHOULD have ended' marker is set up to surface this on the correct day.
context_for_next_testing_agent: MultiDayRunner.js rewrite is sound at the source level — deriveShifts() merges overnight (continuesToNext + continuesFromPrev) into a single shift entry for M2 and M3, while M1 (continuesFromPrev only, prior shift lives in priorDayLog) and M4 (no flags) produce 2 entries each. All required data-testids are present and ShiftQuestionCard / AnotherShiftCard wording is neutral. If live Playwright login starts working again, the test script in iteration_38 thoughts is ready to drop in. Main agent should manual-smoke-test via browser, especially M2 and M3 overnight scenarios (Day 1 18:00 / Day 2 05:30 and Day 1 15:00 / Day 2 06:00) to confirm the runner accepts single cross-midnight answers.
rca of the issue: Not a code issue. Playwright session in this run failed to authenticate (page rendered blank after badge+Continue). Iteration 37 confirmed the same login flow with the same credentials and selectors works. Likely Playwright storage/cookie quirk specific to this run.
